compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is not visible. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but in a state of severe decline/dormancy.

inventory_reconciliation.p1_string_of_nickels: Systemic Loss (Not visible in any frame)

plant_audit.p1: No plant matter detected in yellow pot.
plant_audit.p2: No plant matter detected in black pot.
plant_audit.p3: White rabbit anchor present; plant shows minimal structural integrity.
plant_audit.p4: No plant matter detected in black pot.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dark and potentially hydrophobic; no signs of active growth or moisture.
biome_observations.surface_debris: White granular material (likely perlite or eggshell fragments) present in P3; no fungal activity observed.
biome_observations.desk_environment: Stable, low-light conditions; no external interventions detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows minimal structure; no significant growth observed.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_t2: Introduction of a metallic object (spoon) in T-3 and T-2, suggesting human intervention, then removed.
temporal_deltas.t2_to_current: Return to static, low-visibility state. No recovery or new growth.

visual_health_inference.p3_status: Critical/Dormant. The lack of chlorophyll-rich foliage suggests the specimen is either dead or in a state of extreme stress.
visual_health_inference.overall_biome: The biome is effectively non-functional as a botanical environment.

anomalies: Temporary presence of a spoon in T-3/T-2.
Absence of all registered plants except for the P3 anchor.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical registry. The environment is currently devoid of viable plant life, with the exception of the P3 pot which contains the scale anchor but lacks visible, healthy Pothos foliage. The presence of a spoon in the mid-sequence suggests human interaction, but this has not resulted in any positive botanical development. The biome is currently in a state of total stagnation.
